title: The status of the container is stuck in stopping.
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
I was connecting to one of my containers by ssh,and looking at some code above, then I'm off to eat :(. When I came back after lunch, I found that ssh was disconnected and container was not accessible.
I tried podman stop、podman restart and podman kill but nothing worked.
I guarantee that after I used ssh to connect to the container, I only looked at some code and did not run any programs. **My appeal is how to make the status of this container no longer stop, so that I can use it again.**
Thank you!
**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.Use ssh to connect to a container:
ssh username@IP -p container‘s port
2.go to lunch :(

**Describe the results you received:**
The status of this container is always stopping.
![75@)3~9NHGYFQGHTWYG0_NQ](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/43242755/204189694-d9c96ef1-b611-4264-a0ab-9283738e2ecc.png)
xx represents my container id.
podman stop xx
Error: can only stop created or running containers. xx is in state stopping: container state improper

podman restart xx
Error: unable to restart a container in a paused or unknown state: container state improper

podman kill xx
ERRO[0000] container not running                        
Error: error sending signal to container xx : `/usr/bin/runc kill xx 9` failed: exit status 1
**Describe the results you expected:**
The status of the container is stopped or running

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 4.0.0-dev
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```

  cpus: 80

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @wqshr12345!

There were a number of fixes in that area since Podman 4.0.  If possible, I recommend upgrading to a newer version of Podman.  Version v4.3 should be in CentOS Stream by now.

In case you find an easier reproducer (I've only lunch per day :), please drop it here.  For now, I am going to close the issue as I believe it has been fixed.

This still happens for rootless when `conmon` is unexepectedly stopped. This can either be by linger or when the process crashes upon a system resume. Have been able to reproduce on different environments; a remote VM or a thinkpad tablet.
@gbraad can you be more specific about the exact behavior?  If `conmon` gets killed, the container can remain in the "stopping" state but a `podman stop` or `rm/kill` should clean it up.

Issuing a stop command didn't do anything. I had to rm -f the container. But that sounds like a bad workaround. A start is impossible as it is in the wrong state. Reboot was the only thing that recovered the situation without having to remove the container.
Thanks, I will reopen given there's no PR linked to the issue.
I'll prepare a fix after lunch :heavy_check_mark:

OK, I thought to have seen a wrong check but Podman allows for stopping a container in the "stopping" state to avoid such a situation.

@gbraad if you run into this situation again, please collect debug logs via `podman --log-level=debug`.  Access to the machine may help as well.

Hit this today. Have podman version 4.2.0, the server was rebooted, and afterwards running `podman ps` yielded:

> ERRO[0000] invalid internal status, try resetting the pause process with "podman system migrate": could not find any running process: no such process

Running `podman system migrate` fixed that, but now the containers are now all in "stopping" state, can't start them, can't stop them.

Force remove and recreation worked of course.

I have a similar issue. I'll try out a few stuff and keep you informed, but the basics are:

- BTRFS filesystem
- Every day at 2am:
  - I stop all containers
  - I `btrfs subvolume snapshot`
  - I start all containers

Error happens only on some containers on last step where I can't start container back with `container state improper`. 

Just stopping the container at any time seems to reproduce the problem. 

To fix issue until next stop, only solution is a VM reboot.

Cheers!

EDIT: Forgot to mention `podman ps -a` shows those containers as `stopping`

I am encountering this issue doing coincidentally a very similar thing to @lainwir3d, except instead of a btrfs snapshot I'm using restic to create an offsite backup of a nextcloud instance and its database. I did change my graphroot from the default to a raid1 btrfs with two drives, but that's about it.

The script is run by a systemd timer and it's basically just:

```
podman pod stop nextcloud
podman run ... restic/restic ...
podman pod start nextcloud
```

The backup container mounts the same volumes as the netxcloud containers (using :ro) in order to back them up. This happens both with/without SELinux, podman version is 4.6.2.

The only container that gets stuck is the nextcloud app, not the mariadb or redis containers.

If anyone wants to try reproducing it, this is just a standard nextcloud/mariadb/redis docker install using the latest tag for all three containers, and running on top of opensuse microos.

EDIT: weirdly, I just did a reboot and the container that was stuck at 'Stopping' now shows the status as 'Created' while all the others are 'Exited ... minutes ago'. Idk if that's a clue, or a consequence of spamming `podman kill/stop/restart` a bunch of times before the reboot?
Just after my last message, I did a `podman system migrate` and issue has not reproduced yet (ie. it's been fine for 4 days). I'll keep you informed if this good strike goes on.

I have a unkillable container (even after reboot)

```
[buildbot@bb-rhel8-docker ~]$ podman version

```

```
podman ps -a

Actually this ^ was a big mistake.

I ended up doing a buildah clean up and then podman cleanup and that seemed to clear it.
@mheon, I find this one tricky.  It seems to happen on multiple sites but without a clear reproducer AFAICS.

I have a container, where something which looks awfully like this happens 100%. It is created with this distrobox definition:

```
[vis]
image="registry.opensuse.org/home/mcepl/moldavite/containers/opensuse/vis-master:latest"
pull=true
replace=true
```

If use it as my `$EDITOR` through this script:

```
#!/bin/sh
# distrobox_binary
# name: vis
if [ -z "${CONTAINER_ID}" ]; then
        exec /usr/bin/distrobox-enter  -n vis --                 /usr/bin/vis  "$@"
elif [ -x "/usr/bin/vis" ] ; then
    /usr/bin/vis "$@"
elif [ -x "/usr/bin/distrobox-host-exec" ] ; then
    /usr/bin/distrobox-host-exec ~/.bin/vis "$@"
else
    echo "Cannot run vis"
    exit 255
fi
```

Whenever I want to upgrade the container (`distrobox assemble create -n vis`) it gets stuck. See also https://github.com/89luca89/distrobox/issues/1301

It could be also related to https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/19629

Using podman-5.0.1-1.1.x86_64, distrobox-1.7.1-1.2.noarch, on OpenSUSE MicroOS (with OpenSUSE/Tumbleweed packages), the definition of the container is from https://build.opensuse.org/package/show/home:mcepl:moldavite/vis-master-container

Also tracked in #19629 closing in favor of that one.
Fix incoming in 5.1 regardless.
